2603.29541.title: Can LLM Agents Identify Spoken Dialects like a Linguist?
2603.29541.paper_summary: Due to the scarcity of labeled dialectal speech, audio dialect classification is a challenging task for most languages, including Swiss German. In this work, we explore the ability of large language models (LLMs) as agents in understanding the dialects and whether they can show comparable performance to models such as HuBERT in dialect classification. In addition, we provide an LLM baseline and a human linguist one. Our approach uses phonetic transcriptions produced by ASR systems and combines them with linguistic resources such as dialect feature maps, vowel history, and rules. Our findings indicate that, when linguistic information is provided, the LLM predictions improve. The human baseline shows that automatically generated transcriptions can be beneficial for such classifications, but also presents opportunities for improvement.
2603.29541.paper_summary_zh: 由於標記方言語音的稀缺，音頻方言分類對於大多數語言來說都是一項具有挑戰性的任務，包括瑞士德語。在這項工作中，我們探討大型語言模型（LLMs）作為理解方言的代理的能力，以及它們是否能在方言分類中顯示出與HuBERT等模型相當的性能。此外，我們提供了一個LLM基準和一個人類語言學家的基準。我們的方法使用由ASR系統生成的語音轉錄，並將其與語言資源相結合，例如方言特徵圖、元音歷史和規則。我們的研究結果表明，當提供語言信息時，LLM的預測會有所改善。人類基準顯示，自動生成的轉錄對於這類分類是有益的，但也顯示出改進的機會。
2603.29541.author: Tobias Bystrich et.al.
2603.29541.authors: Tobias Bystrich, Lukas Hamm, Maria Hassan, Lea Fischbach, Lucie Flek, Akbar Karimi
